The hour is Calm, and it reads her differently: zeal cools into serenity, her famous worry stands named at last as the arm's vice, Fear, and Mercury's lists are kept the water-bearer's way, a measured, quiet pouring in the dark. Astraea, last of the immortals to leave the earth, now holds the last watch before sunrise, Spica the one grain still bright at four o'clock.
variants.light_south: In the southern light palette her arm glows blue (#0040FF), a primary of both worlds, but here it is beam rather than pigment. The gleaner becomes the night's last lamp: precision as vigil, worry burned down to watchfulness, her exactness emitted steadily into the hour before dawn, serene because everything has already been counted.

Libra.base:
[[The Sky]] Libra is the only sign that is an object rather than a creature: a balance. It opens on 23 September at the autumn equinox, when day and night stand exactly equal and the year performs its own act of weighing, and it holds the wheel from 90 to 120 degrees, easing the light down into October.

[[The Plate]] The owner draws the idea honestly: a man on one knee, one arm raised, holding the two pans level from a single point of the beam. The glyph below is a sun resting on the horizon line, neither risen nor set.

[[On the Dial]] It rides the red Friday arm of Venus, whose mood is Passion, whose virtue is Love and whose vice is Jealousy. Scorpio shares the arm with it, two schools of the same fire, one weighing passion and one plunging into it.

[[The Lesson]] Its classical ruler is Venus herself, and Venus lives on this very arm. Libra is thus the rare sign seated in its ruler's own house: love judged in the home of love.

variants.paint: In the paint world Libra's arm wears the deep pigment red of #B60000, a crimson with the weight of earth in it, the painter's primary. Passion becomes material: a weight set in the pan, love measured the way goods are weighed at a Friday market, heavy, warm and real.
variants.light: In the light palette the arm burns pure additive red, #FF0000. Red is a primary of light just as it was of paint, so Libra keeps its color across both worlds; only its substance changes. Screen red is not a pigment but a signal, passion as flame rather than clay, and the balance is held in a beam.
variants.paint_south: South of the equator the wheel turns and the balance changes houses: Libra leaves Venus' red evening for Saturn's morning arm, wearing green (#007E00), the pigment of dew and first grass, in the hour called Renewal. Its own equinox flips with it — 23 September opens spring there, not autumn — so the glyph's sun on the horizon is at last rising. The weigher becomes a sower, leveling seed against soil instead of goods against coin, and the seat keeps the old axis' promise: Patience is the very virtue that heals Jealousy.
variants.light_south: In the southern light palette the arm cools to cyan (#00DDDD), the color of the clear sky just before sunrise. The scale's beam becomes a beam in earnest: day measured against night at the year's own dawn, and love judged as a guest in Saturn's house, where patience rather than passion holds the pans level.

Scorpio.base:
[[The Sky]] Scorpio guards the dying of the year, 23 October to 21 November, from 120 to 150 degrees, where autumn stops pretending and the wheel sinks toward the winter tip of the dial. Its stars are drawn as a long chain that fans open at the head and hooks upward at the sting.

[[The Plate]] The owner shows the animal from above, both pincers open and raised, the plated body squared, the segmented tail curled to one side like a weapon sheathed but ready. Even the glyph is an M that ends in a barbed arrow.

[[On the Dial]] It sits on the red Friday arm of Venus, mood Passion, virtue Love, vice Jealousy, and no sign knows that vice more intimately, for jealousy is love that has armed itself. Libra shares the arm: what Libra weighs, Scorpio defends.

[[The Lesson]] Its classical ruler is Mars, who lives across the dial on the orange Tuesday arm. Scorpio is therefore war lodged in the house of love, passion with a soldier's memory.

variants.paint: In paint the arm is the dark pigment red of #B60000, closer to dried blood than to fire. It suits the scorpion: subtractive color works by absorbing light and giving little back, and Scorpio's passion is exactly that, taken in deeply, held, and revealed only when the tail decides.
variants.light: In the light palette the arm blazes as pure #FF0000, the first primary of the additive world. What was venom becomes voltage: passion emitted instead of absorbed, the warning coloration of the screen age, a red that does not wait in shadow but announces itself at full brightness.
variants.paint_south: South of the equator the wheel turns and Scorpio crosses its own healing axis: lifted from the red evening house of Venus, it lands on Saturn's morning arm, wearing green (#007E00) under the mood of Renewal. Its dates now open the spring instead of closing the autumn, and Patience, the star's prescribed cure for Jealousy, is here the arm's own virtue, so the southern scorpion sits inside its medicine. The animal molts as the year does, shedding its plated armor the way the field sheds winter; Mars' iron is beaten into Cronus' sickle, and the venom is held back like sap, war's memory turned to the slow work of growing things.
variants.light_south: In the southern light palette the same arm shines cyan (#00DDDD), the clear sky before sunrise. The scorpion's warning red is gone entirely: venom cools into dew, and what was a threat displayed becomes a signal of first light. Passion is not extinguished but timed, held to the patient frequency of dawn, so the southern Scorpio stings only as the morning does — sharp, cold and renewing.

Sagittarius.base:
[[The Sky]] Sagittarius takes the year's final descent, 22 November to 21 December, from 150 degrees to the bottom of the dial. Its far edge is the winter solstice: the archer walks the year into its midnight.

[[The Plate]] The owner's centaur rears on its hind legs with the bow at full draw, the arrow leveled and calm in mid-leap, aim held steady while the body plunges. Its constellation is the wheel's densest knot of stars, lines crossing like a drawn net.

[[On the Dial]] It stands with Capricorn on the purple Wednesday arm of Mercury: mood Sorrow, virtue Wisdom, vice Greed. Two climbers of the dark season share that post, one aiming beyond it and one enduring through it.

[[The Lesson]] Its ruler Jupiter lives on the yellow Thursday arm at the top of the dial, directly opposite, and by the star's law the opposite virtue heals the vice: Generosity is the cure for Greed. Like its glyph, the archer aims straight up at its ruler's noon.

variants.paint: In paint the arm wears purple, #542E85, red and blue pigment folded together into the dye of mourning and of kings. Sorrow becomes a worn material, and the archer's wisdom reads like Tyrian cloth: costly, dark, earned a drop at a time from the world itself.
variants.light: In the light palette the purple becomes magenta, #BD00BD, the one hue no rainbow contains, invented by the eye where the spectrum's far ends meet. A fitting midnight for an archer: he aims at a point that exists only because the mind insists on joining what nature keeps apart.
variants.paint_south: South of the equator the wheel turns and the archer lands where its aim always pointed: the noon arm of Jupiter, home at last on its own ruler's post, with Joy for its hour instead of Sorrow. In paint the arm wears yellow (#F8E600), the palette's unmixed primary, and the wisdom the north earned drop by drop is here spent freely: the arrow becomes a gift, generosity a form of marksmanship, and the sign's one shadow is Excess, the bow drawn a breath too far.
variants.light_south: In the light palette the southern noon glows green (#00DC00), the prism's own primary at the eye's keenest wavelength. The archer's hour turns from Sorrow to Joy and its aim sharpens: no shot now at an invented midnight color but a level arrow through living light, and only its old seat of Wisdom, straight across the dial, holds the draw short of Excess.

Capricorn.base:
[[The Sky]] Capricorn opens at the exact bottom of the dial. Its first point is the winter solstice, 22 December, the year's midnight beneath the silver tip, and from there to 19 January, 180 to 210 degrees, it does the hardest work on the wheel: turning descent into climb.

[[The Plate]] The owner's beast is the old sea-goat, backswept ridged horns, bearded chin, forelegs lifted mid-climb, and no hindquarters at all, only a coiled fish tail with its fluke raised like a whale sounding. Half of it climbs; half remembers the deep.

[[On the Dial]] It shares the purple Wednesday arm of Mercury with Sagittarius: mood Sorrow, virtue Wisdom, vice Greed. Where the archer aims out of the dark, the goat simply keeps walking through it.

[[The Lesson]] Its ruler is Saturn, keeper of limits and of time, at home on the green Saturday arm whose virtue is Patience. That placement is Capricorn's whole doctrine: the summit is never taken, it is outlasted.

variants.paint: In paint the arm holds pigment purple, #542E85, red and blue ground into the color of winter dusk and old authority. It fits the goat well: sorrow worn as a working coat, wisdom built up in layers like slow glaze, matte, dark and utterly permanent.
variants.light: The light palette turns the arm magenta, #BD00BD, the impossible color the eye conjures where red and blue light meet. The climber's midnight goes electric, sorrow with a neon edge, and the stubborn ascent is lit like a summit beacon no rainbow ever includes.
variants.paint_south: South of the equator the wheel turns and the sea-goat opens at the very top of the dial: its solstice is midsummer there, the year's midnight become its noon. The climber wakes on Jupiter's Thursday arm wearing primary yellow (#F8E600), joy laid on as thick as unmixed paint, and the old doctrine shifts a degree: the summit is no longer outlasted but inhabited, Saturn's hard-won thrift schooled into Generosity, the hoard of the whole climb spent open-handed on the view, wary now only of Excess, the one vice a goat that finally owns the height must learn to fear.
variants.light_south: In the light palette the southern noon glows pure green (#00DC00), the prism's primary at the eye's keenest wavelength, and the same green Saturn's own Saturday arm wears in paint, so the goat at last climbs under its ruler's color. Joy sharpens into growth: not the glare of the summit but the grass that takes it, patience finally in flower, gladness emitted as living signal.

Aquarius.base:
[[The Sky]] Aquarius holds deep winter, 20 January to 18 February, the arc from 210 to 240 degrees on the dial's lower left: the small hours of the year, past midnight and leaning toward dawn, when the light grows even as the cold deepens.

[[The Plate]] The owner's figure kneels on one knee, the great jar tipped at the shoulder, and the water leaves it in scrolled curls, wave folding over wave, the doubled wave the glyph abbreviates to two zigzag lines. An air sign, yet all it owns it pours away as water.

[[On the Dial]] It rests on the blue Monday arm of the Moon: mood Calm, virtue Serenity, vice Fear. Pisces shares the arm, the pourer and the swimmers, the vessel and what lives in the stream.

[[The Lesson]] Its classical ruler is Saturn, at home on the green Saturday arm of Patience. That is the water-bearer's secret: the pouring looks like abandon but is discipline, a measured emptying, serenity practiced until it becomes a public work.

variants.paint: In paint the arm is primary blue, #002FFF, the lapis and indigo of the old workshops, once weighed out like treasure. Calm becomes a substance: the water-bearer pours a color painters ground by hand, and serenity settles onto the year like pigment into cloth.
variants.light: In light the arm keeps its name, for blue is a primary of both worlds, but #0040FF glows instead of covering. The poured water becomes a stream of screen-light, the beam rather than the pigment, and the bearer's calm turns luminous, a steady lamp in the dial's dark quarter.
variants.paint_south: South of the equator the wheel turns, and the water-bearer kneels in high summer, on the 16h arm of Mars, wearing ember orange (#DC9600), the milled pigment of the working afternoon. Deep winter's calm is left in the north; here the jar is carried to the fire, and Saturn's measured emptying becomes the quenching stroke — water poured with a soldier's timing, Zeal in the form of a steady hand, dousing Wrath before the furnace weeks can spill it.
variants.light_south: In the light palette the southern arm burns pure yellow (#FFFF00), red and green summed toward the Sun's own brightness. The poured stream goes incandescent: not the cool Monday beam of the north but water made lamplight, the bearer's discipline emitted as zeal, a working flame that pours without ever burning dry.

Pisces.base:
[[The Sky]] Pisces is the zodiac's closing word, 19 February to 20 March, from 240 to 270 degrees. Its far edge lands exactly on the spring equinox, at the dial's dawn side; there the old year dissolves and hands the wheel on.

[[The Plate]] The owner draws the two fish as a closed ring, each chasing the other's tail like a current with no beginning; the glyph unbinds them into two facing crescents held by one bar. In the star chart one line ends in a small circlet of stars, a fish caught mid-turn.

[[On the Dial]] It lies with Aquarius on the blue Monday arm of the Moon: mood Calm, virtue Serenity, vice Fear. The bearer pours the stream; the fish are what it carries.

[[The Lesson]] Its classical ruler is Jupiter, resident of the yellow Thursday arm of Joy and Generosity. The dial gives the dreamer a benefactor: the sign that ends the year in blue calm is governed from the arm of gladness, so every ending is financed by joy.

variants.paint: Paint gives the arm true pigment blue, #002FFF, the deep working blue of water painted rather than water seen. The two fish swim in a material sea, calm laid on in slow opaque strokes, and even their fear sinks quietly into the same jar of color.
variants.light: In the light palette the arm stays blue, a primary in both worlds, but sharpens into the glowing #0040FF of a screen's own depth. The ring of fish becomes a circuit, two currents of light chasing each other, and the Moon's painted water is remade as pure signal.
variants.paint_south: South of the equator the wheel turns and the fish surface at 16h, on the ember orange (#DC9600) arm of Mars where the maiden gleans in the north. The working afternoon puts the dreamers to work: the closed ring turns like a mill-wheel now, the current drives it instead of carrying it, and Jupiter's generosity is paid out as the sea's own harvest, the netted catch beside the standing grain. Calm warms into Zeal here, and even the old fear is spent as fuel — the swimmer keeps stroke because stopping is the one thing it dreads.
variants.light_south: In the southern light palette the same seat flares to pure yellow (#FFFF00), red and green light summed, the prism's reading of the ember. The ring of fish becomes a wheel of signal, two currents driving each other like a turbine, and the sign that closes the year in northern calm here powers it home — zeal emitted rather than burned.

zodiac_articles_note: zodiac_articles[SignName] = {base, variants}: base = 3-4 left-aligned paragraphs weaving the sign's myth, the owner's art, its season arc on the year wheel, the hexa arm it sits on (color, mood, virtue, vice), its arm partner and its classical ruler's place on the dial; variants = one paragraph each for the paint and light palettes (signs live on the hexa pointer only). Display location TBD by the owner.

Faith.base:
[[The Figure]] Faith is the vertical line of the three: not a feeling but a direction, the part of a person that points upward the way the M points from the top of this ring. Paul named it first of the trio that remains when everything else passes - faith, hope and love - and the dial gives it the highest arm.

[[On the Dial]] It wears yellow, the noon color, and guards the day's height from 8 to 16 - the working hours, which is the quiet joke of its placement: faith is not kept at midnight vigils but in the plain middle of the day, where work either honors what one believes or betrays it.

[[The Lesson]] Two days carry it together. Jupiter's Thursday brings the Priest - faith professed, taught, celebrated - and Saturn's Saturday brings the Farmer's patience: faith TESTED, the kind that sows and waits without proof. Between them the pair warns of its own shadows, Excess and Envy: belief turned loud, or turned sour at a neighbor's harvest.

Hope.base:
[[The Figure]] Hope is the horizon line: it looks not up but FORWARD, at what is coming and cannot yet be seen. The dial seats it on the blue arm at 4h - the hour before dawn, the coldest and darkest stretch of the night, which is exactly where hope does its work.

[[On the Dial]] It guards the morning third, 0 to 8: the hours when the day exists only as a promise. Its blue is the pre-dawn sky one shade before it lightens - Calm, the mood of that hour, because hope is not excitement but a steady expectation.

[[The Lesson]] The Moon's Monday and Mercury's Wednesday share the arm: the Moon lends Serenity, the lamp that holds through the night, and Mercury - the morning star, the herald - lends the message that the light is already on its way. Their shadows are Fear and Greed: the Moon's darkness read as threat instead of promise, and the Merchant's grasping at what the morning has not yet delivered. Hope answers both - it waits, and it waits open-handed.

Love.base:
[[The Figure]] Love is the horizontal line, the one that runs from person to person - and Paul's verdict stands over the whole trio: the greatest of these is love. The dial gives it the red arm at 20h, where Y closes the ring's word, and the evening third from 16 to midnight: the hours people finally turn toward each other.

[[On the Dial]] Its red is Passion, the mood of the evening - the color Venus has always worn, the first star lit in the darkening sky. Love guards the night not as danger but as intimacy: the third of the day that belongs to closeness, tables, beds and long talks.

[[The Lesson]] Venus' Friday and Mars' Tuesday share the arm - the oldest pairing there is, love and courage, the lover and the defender; Babylon knew them as one goddess. Their shadows are Jealousy and Wrath, love's two failures, and the canon sends their cures from across the dial: Patience quells Jealousy, Serenity quells Wrath.

trio_articles_note: trio_articles[virtue] = {base}: one left-aligned multi-paragraph article per Trinity arm (Faith 12h yellow, Hope 4h blue, Love 20h red), shown under the trio arm hover header. The trio has a single palette, so there are no per-style variants.

Rat.base:
[[The Figure]] First of the twelve — the Rat won the Great Race by riding the Ox's head across the river and leaping ashore at the last moment. Its years reward what the story praises: wit, thrift, quickness, the talent of arriving first with the least force.

[[The Plate]] The owner's medallion gives the whole animal, seated and alert, wearing an ornate collar with a lion-faced clasp — a small creature in armor too grand for it, which is exactly the joke and the point. A Rat year returns every twelve; each return wears a new element of the five, so the same year comes back only once in sixty.

Ox.base:
[[The Figure]] The Ox carried the Rat across the river and came second — the zodiac's patron of patient strength, of work done slowly and completely. Ox years favor endurance, honesty and the long furrow over the quick harvest.

[[The Plate]] The medallion shows the great head with curved horns and a meander-patterned yoke on its neck, a bell hanging from the cord and grain stalks in its mouth, a ripe ear of wheat beside it — the harvest it earns. Each twelve-year return arrives in a new element; the same Ox comes back once in sixty years.

Tiger.base:
[[The Figure]] The Tiger is the zodiac's raw courage — the mountain king whose years are said to bring boldness, upheaval and protection all at once; children born under it were dressed in tiger caps against evil spirits.

[[The Plate]] The owner's bronze shows the head in profile, the layered mane combed like flame and a collar of engraved scales — armor worn as calmly as fur. A Tiger year returns every twelve, each time in a new element of the five; the same Tiger, sixty years apart, is said to be a different beast.

Rabbit.base:
[[The Figure]] The Rabbit is the zodiac's gentleness and diplomacy — in the old tale it crossed the river hopping stone to stone and on a drifting log, arriving fourth on grace alone. Its years favor tact, mercy and quiet luck; the Chinese see a rabbit, not a man, in the face of the full Moon, pounding the elixir of life.

[[The Plate]] The medallion holds the head in profile with tall listening ears and a mantle of feather-like scales down the neck — softness given armor. Each twelve-year return comes in a new element; one full circle of the same Rabbit takes sixty years.

Dragon.base:
[[The Figure]] The Dragon is the only creature of the twelve that does not walk the earth — the emperor's own sign, bringer of rain, the year in which births were always said to multiply. Dragon years promise ambition, luck and weather in every sense.

[[The Plate]] The owner's medallion is the fiercest of the set: horned head, flowing whisker-manes, the jaw open around teeth and live EMBERS — sparks caught in bronze — with a smaller dragon curled as the clasp of its collar. Every twelfth year it returns in a new element; the same Dragon flies once in sixty.

Snake.base:
[[The Figure]] The Snake is the zodiac's wisdom in its stillest form — the small dragon, as the Chinese call it, whose years favor insight, elegance and holding one's counsel. It came sixth by hiding on the Horse's hoof and slipping ahead at the line.

[[The Plate]] The medallion coils the whole body into the circle, every coil engraved with its own pattern like damascened steel, the tongue tasting the air between stylized clouds. Each return wears a new element, and no Snake repeats inside sixty years.

Horse.base:
[[The Figure]] The Horse is the zodiac's free runner — years of motion, independence and open roads. The old books warn and promise the same thing: nothing holds a Horse year still — and each return wears a different element, so no two Horse years run alike.

[[The Plate]] The owner's medallion rears the whole animal off the ground, mane and tail streaming like banners, a floral-engraved saddle blanket on its back and the character for horse stamped on its chest roundel, clouds breaking under the hooves. Twelve years to the next Horse; sixty until the same one returns.

Goat.base:
[[The Figure]] The Goat (or Sheep) is the zodiac's peace — years of art, kindness and community, the flock over the race. Eighth of the twelve, and eight is the luckiest of Chinese numbers; a Goat year is said to soften whatever it touches.

[[The Plate]] The medallion gives the bearded head with long ridged horns swept back, a bell on the collar and grain stalks in its mouth, a full ear of wheat beside it — the gentle grazer amid plenty. Each twelve-year return arrives in a new element; the same Goat returns once in sixty years.

Monkey.base:
[[The Figure]] The Monkey is the zodiac's cleverness unchained — the sign of invention, mischief and escape from every trap, with Sun Wukong, the Monkey King who out-tricked heaven itself, as its eternal patron. Monkey years favor wit over strength and improvisation over plans.

[[The Plate]] The owner's bronze stares straight out with a crown of flame-like hair and holds a curling, orb-tipped wand in its teeth with sparks flying — the trickster mid-trick — a small monkey face grinning from its collar medallion. Every twelfth year in a new element; one full Monkey circle takes sixty.

Rooster.base:
[[The Figure]] The Rooster is the zodiac's herald — the one animal whose job is the DAWN, which suits a clock better than any other sign. Its years favor candor, punctuality and hard-earned show: the rooster struts because it works before everyone wakes.

[[The Plate]] The medallion carries the head in profile, the serrated comb worn like a crown and the feathers falling in an engraved cascade down the neck. A Rooster year returns every twelve, each time in one of the five elements — the same Rooster, in the same coat, crows once in sixty years.

Dog.base:
[[The Figure]] The Dog is the zodiac's loyalty — years of justice, honesty and guarding what one loves. Eleventh in the race only because it stopped to play in the river, which the tale counts in its favor: faithfulness never hurries.

[[The Plate]] The owner's medallion shows the whole dog standing guard, curled tail high, a decorated harness on its back and the character for dog on its collar disc, stylized clouds around it. Each twelve-year return comes in a new element; the same Dog keeps its watch once in every sixty years.

Pig.base:
[[The Figure]] The Pig closes the twelve — the zodiac's sincerity and abundance, the sign of enjoying what the other eleven chased. Last in the Great Race because it stopped to eat and sleep, and unashamed of either; Pig years favor generosity, comfort and honest pleasure.

[[The Plate]] The medallion may be the warmest of the set: the round, smiling animal in a richly engraved mantle of armor, the character for pig on its shoulder disc and a piglet-face pendant at its chest. Every twelfth year in a new element — the feast comes back once in sixty years.

chinese_articles_note: chinese_articles[Animal] = {base}: two left-aligned paragraphs per animal, written from the owner's bronze medallions (Greek-key borders — the same frame family as the ring letters), shown under the octa chinese-slot hover with the medallion on top.

Wood.base: [[The Figure]] Wood is the element of spring and the east — growth, flexibility, the green force that splits stone by patience alone. A Wood year bends rather than breaks and builds upward like a tree ring by ring; in the cycle of the five, Wood feeds Fire — what grows becomes fuel for what burns.

Fire.base: [[The Figure]] Fire is the element of summer and the south — passion, visibility, transformation. A Fire year burns brighter and faster than the same animal in any other coat: it warms, illuminates and consumes, and asks only whether the flame is a hearth or a wildfire. In the cycle Fire leaves ash — and ash becomes Earth.

Earth.base: [[The Figure]] Earth is the element of the center and the turning of the seasons — stability, nurture, the ground every other element returns to. An Earth year steadies its animal: harvests are gathered, houses are built, promises are kept. In the cycle Earth bears Metal, the way ore waits inside the mountain.

Metal.base: [[The Figure]] Metal is the element of autumn and the west — precision, rigor, the blade and the bell. A Metal year cuts away what is unnecessary and keeps what rings true: harvest, judgment, craft. In the cycle Metal collects Water, as dew gathers on a cold blade at dawn.

Water.base: [[The Figure]] Water is the element of winter and the north — depth, wisdom, the strength of yielding. A Water year flows around what it cannot move and wears it away all the same; it favors listening, memory and the long view. In the cycle Water feeds Wood, and the wheel of the five begins again.

chinese_elements_note: chinese_elements[Element] = {base}: one paragraph per Wu Xing element, shown under the animal article in the chinese-slot hover (the element qualifies THIS return of the animal; 12 animals x 5 elements = the sexagenary 60).
